scenarios.scenario: 1 — electron-store for 'desktop-plugin-config' fails to initialize (disk I/O error)
scenarios.root_cause: getStore('desktop-plugin-config') → new Store({...}) throws synchronously because app.getPath('userData') is on a read-only filesystem, disk is full, or permissions are wrong. The error is not caught by the IPC handler (lines 377-386 of ipc.ts have no try/catch).
scenarios.signature: Renderer: dialog-manage-plugins shows empty 'No plugins installed' state. Install dialog shows generic 'Failed to install plugin'. Main process: Store constructor writes EACCES/ENOSPC to stderr but no structured logging. No console.error wrapper in the handler.
trace: → Synchronous throw propagates as IPC rejection
→ Renderer loadConfigs catch block: setConfigs([]) — silent swallow
→ OR submitInstall catch block: setError('Failed to install plugin')
→ DevTools console: no error (caught silently in renderer)
scenarios.log_trail: Main process stderr if launched from terminal — otherwise invisible. No app-side log file written. No console.error in the handler. Developer must add logging to the handler or catch block to see the error.
scenarios.verdict: The IPC handler at line 377 lacks any try/catch. Store construction errors are invisible to the renderer. The renderer catch blocks silently fall back to empty state. An experienced developer familiar with the codebase needs ~15 min to add console.error to the handler; a new contributor needs 35+ min to trace from 'No plugins installed' to a disk permission error.
scenarios.fix: Wrap ipcMain.handle('get-desktop-plugin-config') and 'set-desktop-plugin-config' in try/catch with console.error and structured error return. Wrap getStore() calls in the handler body, not outside.
detection_methods: Launch electron from terminal → stderr shows Store error
Check ~/Library/Application Support/ai.opencode.desktop.dev/ for config file corruption/deletion
Add console.error to the renderer's catch blocks

scenarios.scenario: 2 — IPC call times out or hangs
scenarios.root_cause: ipcMain.handle has no timeout. ipcRenderer.invoke returns a Promise that never settles if the main process handler hangs (e.g., store.set synchronous write blocks on NFS lock, or getStore constructor deadlocks on a corrupted config file).
scenarios.signature: dialog-manage-plugins: loading spinner never resolves. dialog-install-plugin: 'Installing...' button stays disabled forever. Plugin context: loader.loadAll() never resolves. No error in DevTools console — the await just never completes.
trace: → Main process handler starts but never resolves
→ setLoading(false) in finally block never runs (finally only runs on settle)
→ No console.error, no unhandled rejection, no error boundary catch
scenarios.log_trail: No log trail at all. The promise simply never settles. Developer must discover by: (1) noticing infinite loading, (2) setting a breakpoint in DevTools to verify the await never returns, (3) checking main process for blocking operations.
scenarios.verdict: Silent hang with zero error feedback. The loading state pattern (createSignal(true) → async load → finally setLoading(false)) fails silently because finally never runs. The developer must manually instrument the IPC boundary to add timeouts. No Electron-level timeout exists.
scenarios.fix: Add timeout wrapper around ipcRenderer.invoke (e.g., Promise.race with a setTimeout rejection). Add timeout to ipcMain.handle via AbortSignal or a manual timeout guard.
detection_methods: Look for infinite spinner — doesn't differentiate load vs hang vs empty
Performance tab in DevTools may show pending IPC
Main process debugger breakpoint on handler callback
Only reliable: add a console.log before/after the invoke to see if it never returns

scenarios.scenario: 3 — plugin-loader.ts dynamic import() fails for a plugin path
scenarios.root_cause: loadEntry at plugin-loader.ts:107 calls `await import(entry.path)` with no try/catch. If the path does not exist, is not a valid JS/TS module, or Vite cannot resolve it, the import() rejects. Since loadAll uses Promise.all, a single rejection kills ALL plugin loading.
scenarios.signature: DevTools console: unhandled rejection 'Failed to load module url...' or 'ChunkLoadError' with stack trace pointing to loadEntry:107. DesktopPluginProvider's onMount does `.catch(console.error)` so the error IS logged. But Promise.all means 1 bad plugin → 0 plugins loaded. The manage-plugins dialog still shows the plugin in the list (config read succeeded), but none of the slots render.
trace: → No further entries processed — all plugins fail to load
scenarios.log_trail: Clear: console.error in DesktopPluginProvider.onMount logs the full error with stack trace to DevTools. The error includes the file path and the module resolution error.
scenarios.verdict: The .catch(console.error) in plugin-context.tsx:44 is a good guard — the error surfaces in DevTools quickly. However, Promise.all is the wrong concurrency primitive: one failure kills all plugins. The developer sees one error but 10 non-loading plugins and needs to figure out that the Promise.all domino killed the rest. Time is low because the error message is clear.
scenarios.fix: Replace Promise.all with Promise.allSettled + per-plugin console.warn for failures. Add try/catch around the import() call in loadEntry. Log the plugin name alongside the error.
detection_methods: DevTools console shows the error with stack trace
Stack points directly to loadEntry:107
Plugin name can be correlated from config vs loaded set

scenarios.scenario: 4 — A plugin module doesn't have a desktop() entrypoint
scenarios.root_cause: loadEntry line 108-109: `if (!mod.desktop) return` — completely silent. The module imports successfully but has no desktop export. No error, no warning, no log.
scenarios.signature: Plugin appears in the manage-plugins list (enabled). loadEntry resolves successfully (no error). Plugin slot never renders. DevTools console: NOTHING. No logs anywhere. The plugin simply doesn't work with zero diagnostic output.
trace: → if (!mod.desktop) return — early return, no log
→ Plugin 'loaded' but no slot registered, no dispose callback
→ No console.warn, no telemetry, no visible feedback
scenarios.log_trail: No log trail. Zero output. The developer must add instrumentation or inspect the loaded module manually.
scenarios.verdict: The silent return at line 109 is a debuggability sink. The developer sees: (1) install succeeds, (2) toggle works, (3) nothing happens. They must inspect the module's exports and trace through loadEntry to find 'if (!mod.desktop) return'. A single console.warn would reduce time from 22 min to 2 min.
scenarios.fix: Add console.warn('Plugin ${entry.name} has no desktop() entrypoint; skipping') before the return. Consider surfacing this in the manage-plugins dialog as a status indicator.
detection_methods: Add breakpoint in DevTools at loadEntry line 108
Manually import the module in console and check exports
No automated detection

scenarios.scenario: 5 — dialog-manage-plugins IIFE runs before onMount — timing issue with SolidJS reactivity
scenarios.root_cause: Line 139-140: `;(async () => await loadConfigs())()` runs synchronously during component execution (render phase), not inside onMount. In SolidJS StrictMode (dev), components are double-invoked, spawning two concurrent IPC calls. If the component is conditionally mounted (inside Show), the IIFE fires during every mount cycle, potentially causing duplicate loadConfigs calls that race each other.
scenarios.signature: Dev mode (StrictMode): plugin config loads twice, two concurrent IPC calls. One resolves and sets configs, then the other resolves and overwrites with the same data — harmless but wasteful. Edge case: if unmount + remount happens before first call resolves, the IIFE fires again while the first call is still pending. The component shows loading → data → loading → data flicker. No errors in console.
trace: → Return JSX — Show when={loading()} shows spinner
→ If StrictMode double-invokes: IIFE fires TWICE → 2 IPC calls → first sets configs, second sets same configs
scenarios.log_trail: No error, but the double IPC call can be observed in DevTools performance panel -> IPC tracing. The developer would not normally look for this unless they suspect a performance issue.
scenarios.verdict: This is not actually a bug in production (SolidJS double-invocation is dev-only). The IIFE pattern is safe in SolidJS because signals can be set outside onMount; the reactive system handles updates correctly. The only observable effect is a brief 'Loading...' flash, which is expected async loading behavior. The solid-primitive `createAsync` or Solid Router's `beforeLoad` would be more idiomatic, but the current pattern is functional.
scenarios.fix: Move the loadConfigs call inside onMount() for correctness. Or use createAsync from solid-js with a resource for Suspense integration. Add an abort controller if strict dedup is needed.
detection_methods: Network/performance tab shows duplicate IPC calls (dev-only)
Rare flicker in prod if rapid remount (e.g., tab switching)
Console.group trace if onMount is swapped in

scenarios.scenario: 6 — User enters invalid data in install form (duplicate/invalid specifier/bad path)
scenarios.root_cause: The code has validation for duplicate names (line 103), npm format (line 92), and empty file paths (line 97). However: (a) isValidFilePath is `path.length > 0` — accepts any non-empty string including nonexistent paths, (b) npm validation is format-only (regex) and does not verify the package exists on npm, (c) the duplicate check uses `configs().some(c => c.name === name)` which is case-sensitive — 'MyPlugin' vs 'myplugin' passes as different.
scenarios.signature: User-visible error toasts for format/duplicate violations. For nonexistent file paths: plugin 'installs' (appears in list) but fails during loadEntry import() — see Scenario 3 (logged in DevTools). For case-sensitivity duplicates: two entries for the same plugin with different cases. No error during install.
trace: → Inline error toast appears immediately — CORRECT BEHAVIOR
→ Plugin listed but not loaded — no status indicator
User enters duplicate name with different case 'MYPLUGIN' vs 'myplugin'
→ configs().some(c => c.name === name) returns false (case mismatch)
→ Plugin added as separate entry — DUPLICATE
scenarios.log_trail: Format errors: user-visible toast. File-existence failure: console.error in DevTools from plugin-context catch. Case-sensitivity: no error, two entries visible.
scenarios.verdict: Format validation is user-visible and immediate — good. The file path validation gap (accepting nonexistent paths) is the main concern: it passes install validation but fails at load time. The load error is logged (catch in plugin-context), so the developer sees it in DevTools. Case-insensitive duplicate detection is a missing edge case.
scenarios.fix: Tighten isValidFilePath to check file existence via an IPC call (stat) or at least validate it's an absolute path. Add case-insensitive duplicate check: `configs().some(c => c.name.toLowerCase() === name.toLowerCase())`. Add status indicator per plugin (loaded/failed).
detection_methods: Format/duplicate errors: inline toast in dialog — immediate
Nonexistent path: console.error in DevTools from plugin-context
Case-sensitivity: manual inspection of plugin list shows duplicates

scenarios.scenario: 7 — setDesktopPluginConfig returns successfully but store.set silently fails (partial write)
scenarios.root_cause: electron-store v10 uses asyncWrite: true by default. store.set('configs', value) updates the in-memory cache synchronously but writes to disk asynchronously. The set-desktop-plugin-config handler (ipc.ts:381-386) returns result immediately before the async disk write completes. If the write fails (disk full, permissions revoked mid-write, app crash), the in-memory state and UI show success, but the disk state is stale. On app restart, all changes are lost.
scenarios.signature: During session: everything works. Plugins install, toggle, remove — all appear to persist. DevTools: no errors. After app restart: plugin state reverts to previous good state. Changes made during the last session are gone. No error shown to the user.
trace: → Handler returns result — renderer sees success
→ Async write fails (ENOSPC, EACCES) — electron-store logs to internal console.error
→ No feedback to renderer — no callback, no event, no retry
→ Next launch: store.get('configs') reads stale file from disk
→ All previous session changes are lost
scenarios.log_trail: electron-store version 10 logs async write errors to console.error in the main process — visible if launched from a terminal. No persisting error to a log file by default. No propagation to renderer. No user-facing indication.
scenarios.verdict: This is the most insidious bug. The state appears consistent during the session (monotonic reads from in-memory cache), and the UI reports success. The dev must: (1) notice state loss after restart, (2) reproduce by making changes and killing the process, (3) check the disk files, (4) discover electron-store's async write behavior. 45+ minutes for most developers because they'll initially blame IPC handler registration or state management.
scenarios.fix: Options: (a) Set asyncWrite: false in the Store constructor options at store.ts — makes writes synchronous but blocks IPC, (b) Await store.set's internal write (electron-store v10 set() returns void, but you can call `(store as any)._write()` or listen for errors via the error event emitter), (c) Add an explicit readback after write: store.get() and compare with the expected value, (d) Migrate to SQLite or other transactional storage.
detection_methods: Observe: changes visible in session, gone after restart
Check ~/Library/Application Support/ai.opencode.desktop.dev/desktop-plugin-config file timestamp vs expected
Launch from terminal and look for async write errors in stderr
Add a readback validation after every write
Only reliable: set asyncWrite:false and accept synchronous writes

weak_spots.spot: IPC handlers lack try/catch for store operations — get-desktop-plugin-config and set-desktop-plugin-config handlers (ipc.ts:377-386) have zero error handling around getStore/store.get/store.set calls. Any store failure silently propagates as an IPC rejection that the renderer swallows.
silent return on missing desktop() entrypoint — loadEntry at plugin-loader.ts:108-109 returns without logging when a module has no desktop export. This is a zero-diagnostic failure path.
Promise.all kills all plugins on single import failure — plugin-loader.ts:88: `await Promise.all(...)`. A single bad plugin path prevents all other plugins from loading. No isolation.

renderer catch blocks swallow errors silently — dialog-manage-plugins.tsx:39-41 catch block has no console.error. toggleEnabled/removePlugin catch (lines 53-55, 64-66) silently reload. Only submitInstall shows a generic error toast.

electron-store asyncWrite creates invisible data loss window — store.ts:15-19 uses default asyncWrite:true (electron-store v10). set-desktop-plugin-config returns before the disk write completes. If the write fails, data is silently lost on restart.
no IPC timeout anywhere — ipcRenderer.invoke has no built-in timeout, and the renderer code adds none. Any hang in the main process causes indefinite UI freezing (spinner, 'Installing...' button).

IIFE instead of onMount for data loading — dialog-manage-plugins.tsx:140 uses an IIFE outside onMount. In StrictMode (dev), this double-fires. On rapid remount cycles, this races. SolidJS convention is onMount for side effects.
isValidFilePath is a pass-through — accepts any non-empty string. A nonexistent file path passes install validation and fails silently at load time.
case-sensitive duplicate detection — dialog-manage-plugins.tsx:103 and dialog-install-plugin.tsx:59 use c.name === name (case sensitive). 'Plugin' and 'plugin' are treated as different.

recommendations: Wrap IPC handlers for plugin config in try/catch with console.error (ipc.ts:377-386)
Add console.warn in loadEntry for missing desktop() entrypoint (plugin-loader.ts:109)
Replace Promise.all with Promise.allSettled for plugin loading isolation (plugin-loader.ts:88)
Add console.error to renderer catch blocks (dialog-manage-plugins.tsx:39,53,64)
Set asyncWrite: false in electron-store Store constructor or add readback validation (store.ts:15)
Add 10s timeout wrapper around ipcRenderer.invoke calls in the renderer
Replace IIFE with onMount for data loading (dialog-manage-plugins.tsx:139-140)
Tighten file path validation: require absolute path or run existence check via IPC
Add plugin-level status indicators (loaded/failed/error) in the manage dialog
